site: Carlin East Fluid Bed Roaster A
description: Stack volatile As2O3 emissions spiked to 0.72 mg/Nm3, exceeding the Nevada DEP Title V limit of 0.50 mg/Nm3. The feed ore contained high pyrite and active organic carbon (TOC > 3.5%) (high carbon). The kiln operator attempted to run under low excess air (28%) to save thermal efficiency.
root_cause: Low Excess Combustion Air (under 32%) prevented complete oxidation of arsenic sulfides into stable, non-volatile iron arsenate (FeAsO4), causing arsenic to escape as gaseous As2O3.
corrective_actions: Maintain Excess Air above 33.5% and increase tertiary quench air temperature to ensure legal stack compliance for high-arsenic/high-pyrite feeds.

description: Severe clay sintering occurred in the upper calcining zone, collapsing the roasted ore porosity and reducing downstream cyanide recovery to 68.2%. The bed temperature was operated at 730°C.
root_cause: High clay and carbonate content (carbonates > 8.0%, quartz < 60%) experienced solid-state mineral sintering when roasting wall temperatures exceeded 680°C under low tertiary air quenching.
corrective_actions: Impose a strict roasting wall temperature limit of 680°C for clay-rich argillite ores. Increase quench air flow to reduce calcining wall temps if carbonates exceed 8.0%.

description: Gold recovery fell to 80.4% despite successful sulfide oxidation. The ore contained sub-microscopic organic carbon (TOC) that was not fully deactivated. Corresponds to low carbon
root_cause: Feed rate was too high (110 TPH) and wall temperatures were too low (under 610°C), resulting in incomplete combustion of active carbonaceous matter (preg-robbing TCM). The active carbon adsorbed gold-cyanide complexes during leaching.
corrective_actions: Enforce a minimum roasting temp of 630°C for carbonaceous ores. Reduce feed rate to 95 TPH to increase resident residence time.
